content: State-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agent memory systems can be redefined as nested hierarchies of micro-agents, where each micro-agent independently curates which memory fragments it actively chooses to retain or discard based on local curiosity-driven reward signals, rather than having memory selection controlled by a single centralized agent. This nested micro-agent architecture for active memory outperforms existing monolithic designs by aligning memory retention with the intrinsic usefulness of stored information.
